raw_response:
To France — private and direct

I prefer a frank Anglo‑French understanding rather than a scrape over Calais. My priority is a secure Channel and an uncontested North Sea so my home fleets can guard our trade lanes. Practically: I will keep at least one fleet watching the Channel/North Sea frontier this spring; if Germany pushes KIE→Ska/Den or tries NTH, I will respond to defend the North Sea. In return I ask France to avoid making a first stab west into the Irish Sea or Yorkshire — keep Brest/Paris focused south or neutral.

If we agree, the immediate benefit is mutual safety: I police the northern approaches, you secure your southern flank. That leaves Germany with the uncomfortable choice of turning east or wasting effort at sea. Are you willing to hold Brest/Paris steady this turn in return for my policing of the Channel/North Sea?

To Germany — private and succinct

I favour a quiet northern status quo. I will likely posture a fleet into the North Sea this spring and keep London/Edinburgh ready to contest German naval moves that threaten the Channel or our trade routes. I am prepared to avoid needless clashes if you keep Kiel/Skagerrak/Denmark out of a direct push into the North Sea.

Proposal: you refrain from contesting the Channel/North Sea boundary and I refrain from interfering in your central European objectives. If you instead send fleets into NTH/Helgoland/Skagerrak expect me to contest firmly — timing is in my favour at sea. Does that steadiness suit you?

To Russia — short and candid

You are the largest continental presence. I do not want a single power to run Europe. My fleets can only influence the north and sea lanes, but that is a real constraint on your access to Scandinavia and the Baltic. This spring I will be watching and likely holding a fleet in the North Sea/Skagerrak line — that line is where I can most quickly check naval ambitions.

If you commit to a non‑aggressive posture in Scandinavia/Poland I will not intervene against your land moves. If you press north or threaten to dominate the Balkans, I will look to coordinate with France/Italy/Turkey to contain you. What are your intentions for Scandinavia and Warsaw this year?

To Italy — brief

I prefer flexible agreements. My naval power can blunt German or Russian reach north and can, with favour, convoy against Turkey or keep Austria honest. If you feel pressure from Austria or Turkey, tell me where an English fleet in the Channel/North Sea would be useful as a diversion. I will not back any continental hegemon.

To Austria — short

I will not get involved on the Danube directly, but a strong central Europe worries me if it tips towards a single hegemon. My fleets will shape northern threats. If you need our diplomacy to hold Turkey or Russia in check, say so; I favour flexible, temporary coalitions rather than long land commitments.

To Turkey — concise

I won’t be fighting in the Mediterranean this spring, but I will be signalling strongly in the Channel/North Sea. If you and Austria need a balance against Russia, I can be a willing diplomatic partner; if you plan to press into the Aegean, beware that a continental chain reaction will attract French and Italian attention. I prefer measured moves and clear promises.

Final note to all

My posture this spring will be naval and cautious: one fleet to police the Channel/Channel–North Sea frontier, another ready to contest the North Sea/Skagerrak if necessary, and my Liverpool army kept for coastal defence or opportunistic support. I seek flexible agreements that prevent any single continental power from dominating — open to concrete, reciprocal promises rather than vague assurances. Which of you will make the first clear, reciprocal commitment?
